proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous covariate feature_078 (range 30-90, mean 65, plausibly age or another scalar baseline characteristic) are associated with longer pfs_months.
Patients with feature_051 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051 = 0.
Patients with feature_038 = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_038 = 0 (feature_038 appears treatment-like given its prevalence ~20% and direction of effect on the outcome).

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])  # also linregress for slope
analyses.result_summary: Strong positive linear association between feature_078 and pfs_months: Pearson r = 0.766, p < 1e-300, OLS slope = +0.177 months of PFS per one-unit increase in feature_078.
Mean pfs_months is 3.70 in feature_051=1 (n=27,481) vs 5.05 in feature_051=0 (n=22,519); difference = -1.35 months, Welch t = -68.5, p < 1e-300.
Mean pfs_months is 5.09 in feature_038=1 (n=10,022) vs 4.12 in feature_038=0 (n=39,978); difference = +0.97 months, t = 32.0, p = 1.7e-216.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the ordinal feature_057 (values 0, 1, 2; modal value 1; plausibly an ECOG-like performance score) are associated with shorter pfs_months in a monotone, decreasing-trend manner.
Higher values of the continuous lab-like feature_099 (range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher values of the continuous lab-like feature_092 (range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.80, plausibly serum albumin) are associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[y[df['feature_057']==v] for v in [0,1,2]]); stats.pearsonr(df['feature_057'], y)
analyses.result_summary: Strong monotone decreasing trend in mean pfs_months: 5.27 (feature_057=0), 4.05 (=1), 2.91 (=2); one-way ANOVA F = 3528.9, p < 1e-300; ordinal-trend r = -0.352.
Negative association between feature_099 and pfs_months: r = -0.123, p = 2.2e-167; OLS slope = -0.073 months PFS per unit feature_099.
Positive association between feature_092 and pfs_months: r = 0.102, p = 3.2e-115; OLS slope = +0.465 months PFS per unit feature_092.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_013 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_013 = 0.
Patients with feature_043 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_043 = 0.
Higher values of the continuous lab-like feature_009 (range 0.02-777, heavy right tail, plausibly a tumor marker such as CEA) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Patients with the rare binary feature_067 = 1 (~3% prevalence) have longer pfs_months than feature_067 = 0 patients.
Patients with the rare binary feature_109 = 1 (~5% prevalence) have shorter pfs_months than feature_109 = 0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 4.12 in feature_013=1 (n=20,898) vs 4.45 in feature_013=0 (n=29,102); difference = -0.33, t = -16.1, p = 1.9e-58.
Mean pfs_months 4.11 in feature_043=1 (n=17,657) vs 4.42 in feature_043=0 (n=32,343); difference = -0.31, t = -14.8, p = 1.0e-49.
Weak but highly significant negative association: r = -0.038, p = 3.1e-17; OLS slope = -0.0053 months per unit (small per-unit effect but large dynamic range).
Mean pfs_months 4.53 in feature_067=1 (n=1,506) vs 4.30 in feature_067=0 (n=48,494); difference = +0.22, t = 3.38, p = 7.3e-04.
Mean pfs_months 4.09 in feature_109=1 (n=2,272) vs 4.32 in feature_109=0 (n=47,728); difference = -0.23, t = -5.11, p = 3.5e-07.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_064 (white, hispanic, asian, black, other; plausibly self-reported race/ethnicity).
Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_018 (medicare, private, uninsured, medicaid; plausibly insurance type).
Among patients in feature_064, black patients have shorter pfs_months than white patients (a directional pairwise contrast addressing a clinically relevant disparity question).
Patients with feature_018 = uninsured have shorter pfs_months than feature_018 = private patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_064: white 4.316, hispanic 4.315, asian 4.306, black 4.273, other 4.407; one-way ANOVA F = 0.87, p = 0.479. No global difference.
Mean pfs_months by feature_018: medicare 4.312, private 4.330, medicaid 4.277, uninsured 4.249; one-way ANOVA F = 1.47, p = 0.219. No global difference.
Mean pfs_months 4.316 (white) vs 4.273 (black); difference = +0.04 (white slightly higher, opposite of the directional hypothesis claim that black<white in a meaningful way), t = 1.31, p = 0.189.
Mean pfs_months 4.249 (uninsured) vs 4.330 (private); difference = -0.08, t = -1.54, p = 0.125. Direction matches hypothesis but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, feature_092, feature_099, feature_009, feature_064, and feature_018 in a multivariable OLS model, feature_051 remains independently associated with shorter pfs_months and feature_038 remains independently associated with longer pfs_months.

The race variable feature_064 and the insurance variable feature_018 are NOT independently associated with pfs_months after adjusting for feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, feature_057, and other top covariates.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_057 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_009 + feature_067 + feature_109 + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS (n=50,000, adjusted R² = 0.858). feature_051 adjusted coef = -1.36 (SE 0.008, p<1e-300), feature_038 adjusted coef = +0.94 (SE 0.010, p<1e-300). feature_078 +0.176/unit, feature_057 -1.17/level, feature_092 +0.47, feature_099 -0.076, feature_013 -0.35, feature_043 -0.29, feature_009 -0.005, feature_109 -0.36 — all p<1e-50. ALL feature_064 (race) levels p>0.50 and ALL feature_018 (insurance) levels p>0.21 after adjustment — no independent contribution.
feature_038 adjusted coefficient = +0.943 months (95% CI +0.924 to +0.962), t = 97.8, p << 1e-300, confirming an independent positive association.

analyses.code: model.pvalues[[c for c in model.params.index if 'feature_064' in c or 'feature_018' in c]]
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, p-values for race indicators are 0.92 (black), 0.85 (hispanic), 0.58 (other), 0.51 (white vs reference asian); for insurance indicators 0.54 (medicare), 0.76 (private), 0.22 (uninsured). None reach significance — apparent absence of unadjusted disparities is preserved after multivariable adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit conferred by feature_038 (positive) is modified by the binary biomarker feature_013: patients with feature_013 = 0 derive a larger PFS benefit from feature_038 than patients with feature_013 = 1 (qualitative biomarker × treatment interaction).
The PFS benefit conferred by feature_038 is also modified by the binary biomarker feature_043: feature_038 confers a larger PFS gain in feature_043 = 0 patients than in feature_043 = 1 patients.
feature_038 effect is NOT modified by feature_051 (i.e., the negative prognostic effect of feature_051 is independent of the feature_038 treatment effect).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_013*feature_038', data=df).fit(); plus stratified t-tests
analyses.result_summary: Strong negative interaction: feature_013:feature_038 coef = -1.64 (p < 1e-300). Stratified means: in feature_013=0, feature_038 adds +1.66 months PFS (5.77 vs 4.12, p<1e-292); in feature_013=1, feature_038 adds only +0.03 months (4.14 vs 4.12, p=0.46). Qualitative interaction confirmed.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_043*feature_038', data=df).fit(); stratified t-tests
analyses.result_summary: Strong negative interaction: feature_043:feature_038 coef = -1.45 (p < 1e-300). Stratified: in feature_043=0, feature_038 adds +1.48 months (5.60 vs 4.12, p<1e-273); in feature_043=1, feature_038 adds +0.03 months (4.14 vs 4.11, p=0.52). Same qualitative pattern as feature_013.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051*feature_038', data=df).fit(); stratified t-tests
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p = 0.179 (coef +0.029). Stratified feature_038 effect: +0.94 months in feature_051=0 vs +1.01 months in feature_051=1 — essentially identical absolute treatment effect. feature_051 acts as a pure prognostic factor independent of feature_038 treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A composite biomarker positivity flag (marker_pos = 1 if feature_013 = 1 OR feature_043 = 1; ~62% prevalence) abolishes the feature_038 PFS benefit: feature_038 confers a large PFS gain only in marker_pos = 0 patients (joint biomarker-negative subgroup).

feature_013 and feature_043 are positively correlated with each other (statistically associated rather than independent), suggesting overlapping biology or shared upstream cause; if so, treating them as a composite resistance signature is justifiable.

analyses.code: df['marker_pos'] = ((df['feature_013']==1)|(df['feature_043']==1)).astype(int); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + marker_pos*feature_038', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: marker_pos:feature_038 interaction coef = -2.52 (p < 1e-300). Stratified: marker_pos=0 (n=18,853), feature_038 adds +2.52 months (6.64 vs 4.12, p<1e-300); marker_pos=1 (n=31,147), feature_038 adds +0.04 months (4.16 vs 4.12, p=0.16). Composite biomarker cleanly partitions responders from non-responders.

Crosstab feature_013 x feature_043: (0,0)=18853, (0,1)=10249, (1,0)=13490, (1,1)=7408. Chi-square test of independence p ≈ 0.55 (no association); odds ratio ≈ 1.01. The two markers are essentially statistically independent — composite OR-rule simply increases the prevalence of the resistance signature, not because of shared biology in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 does NOT vary by patient feature_078 (the strong age-like covariate): treatment effect is constant across the feature_078 range.
The PFS benefit of feature_038 is reduced (in absolute terms) at higher levels of feature_057 (the ECOG-like ordinal factor): feature_057 × feature_038 interaction is negative.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078*feature_038 + feature_057 + feature_051', data=df).fit(); stratify by feature_078 tertiles
analyses.result_summary: feature_078:feature_038 coef = +0.0012/unit (p = 0.28, NS). Stratified by feature_078 tertiles: feature_038 effect = +1.01 (low), +0.93 (mid), +0.94 (high) months — essentially flat. Treatment effect is independent of feature_078.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + feature_057*feature_038', data=df).fit(); stratified means
analyses.result_summary: feature_057:feature_038 coef = -0.059 per level (p = 2.5e-04). Stratified: feature_038 adds +0.93 (feature_057=0), +1.00 (=1), +0.90 (=2) months — significant statistically but tiny in absolute magnitude (<0.1 months); feature_038 confers a clinically similar benefit at all feature_057 levels. Direction is opposite the hypothesized larger-attenuation-with-higher-feature_057 pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The probability of receiving feature_038 (presumed therapy) differs by feature_064 (race): non-white patients are less likely than white patients to receive feature_038.
The probability of receiving feature_038 differs by feature_018 (insurance): uninsured/medicaid patients are less likely than privately insured patients to receive feature_038.
Among patients who receive feature_038, the PFS benefit is similar across feature_064 racial categories (no race × treatment interaction).
Among patients who receive feature_038, the PFS benefit is similar across feature_018 insurance categories (no insurance × treatment interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Receipt of feature_038 by race: white 20.0%, hispanic 19.8%, asian 21.1%, black 20.1%, other 19.2%. Chi-square = 3.05, p = 0.55. No disparity in treatment receipt by race in this dataset.
Receipt of feature_038 by insurance: medicare 20.2%, private 20.1%, uninsured 19.0%, medicaid 19.8%. Chi-square = 1.91, p = 0.59. No disparity in treatment receipt by insurance.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + C(feature_064)*feature_038', data=df).fit(); joint F-test for race:feature_038 terms
analyses.result_summary: Per-race feature_038 effect: white +0.92, hispanic +1.11, asian +1.09, black +0.97, other +1.22 months. Individual race × treatment interaction terms all p > 0.14. Joint F-test on the four interaction terms: F = 2.40 on 4 df, p = 0.048 — borderline omnibus signal, but no individual race shows a significantly different treatment effect from the others, and the absolute spread is ~0.3 months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + C(feature_018)*feature_038', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Insurance × feature_038 individual interaction p-values: medicare 0.18, private 0.41, uninsured 0.08, medicaid (reference). No significant insurance × treatment interaction; treatment benefit is consistent across insurance categories.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The composite biomarker × treatment interaction is preserved within feature_051 strata, and within each (marker_pos, feature_051) cell, feature_038 = 1 patients have higher mean pfs_months than feature_038 = 0 patients only when marker_pos = 0.

A consolidated multivariable OLS that includes feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_092, feature_099, and the feature_038 × marker_pos interaction explains a very large fraction of the variance in pfs_months (R² > 0.85), and within this model the feature_038 main-effect coefficient (treatment benefit at marker_pos = 0) is approximately +2.5 months while the feature_038 × marker_pos interaction is approximately -2.5 months (i.e., total feature_038 effect ≈ 0 at marker_pos = 1).
feature_051 does not act as a treatment-effect modifier of feature_038 even when conditioned on marker_pos: feature_051 × marker_pos is null.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way means (months / n): marker_pos=0,feature_051=0: 4.89 (n=6771) vs 7.30 (n=1705) → +2.41 with feature_038; marker_pos=0,feature_051=1: 3.49 (n=8319) vs 6.09 (n=2058) → +2.60; marker_pos=1,feature_051=0: 4.85 (n=11278) vs 4.89 (n=2765) → +0.04; marker_pos=1,feature_051=1: 3.50 (n=13610) vs 3.58 (n=3494) → +0.07. Pattern preserved: large benefit only when marker_pos=0, regardless of feature_051.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_038*marker_pos + feature_092 + feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R² = 0.904, adj R² = 0.904 (n=50,000). feature_038 main coef = +2.516 (SE 0.013, p<1e-300; this is the treatment effect at marker_pos=0). feature_038:marker_pos coef = -2.519 (SE 0.016, p<1e-300; cancels the main effect at marker_pos=1, leaving net ≈ 0). marker_pos main effect alone = +0.001 (p = 0.89). Hypothesis confirmed in both magnitude and direction.

feature_051:marker_pos coefficient = -0.014, p = 0.456. feature_051 acts as a pure prognostic factor — it shortens pfs_months by ~1.36 months independent of marker_pos status and independent of treatment receipt.
